      <li><strong>Surgical oncologist:</strong> Specializes in cancer operations</li>
      <li><strong>Medical oncologist:</strong> Manages chemotherapy and systemic treatments</li>
      <li><strong>Radiation oncologist:</strong> Manages radiation therapy</li>
      <li><strong>Anesthesiologist:</strong> Manages anesthesia and pain during surgery</li>
      <li><strong>Pathologist:</strong> Examines tissue removed during surgery</li>
      <li><strong>Dietitian, physical therapist, social worker, psychologist:</strong> Support your preparation and recovery</li>
    </ul>
    <p>It is important that all team members evaluate your case before treatment begins.</p>

    <h4>Prehabilitation for Cancer Surgery</h4>
    <p>Just as athletes train before a competition, you can train your body before surgery. Studies show prehabilitation can <strong>reduce postoperative complications by up to 40%</strong>.</p>

    <p><strong>Exercise</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>Aim for at least 30 minutes of moderate activity most days (walking, cycling, swimming)</li>
      <li>Even small amounts are better than none</li>
      <li>Your team can design a program tailored to your abilities</li>
    </ul>

    <p><strong>Nutrition</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>Eat plenty of protein to maintain muscle mass</li>
      <li>If you&rsquo;ve lost weight or have a poor appetite, tell your team</li>
      <li>Immunonutrition formulas (arginine, omega-3 fatty acids) may be recommended before major abdominal cancer surgery</li>
    </ul>

    <p><strong>Mental &amp; Emotional Preparation</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>Distress is highest around diagnosis and surgery &mdash; and gradually improves</li>
      <li>Deep breathing, meditation, guided imagery, and support groups all help</li>
      <li>Your cancer center should screen you for distress and connect you with resources</li>
    </ul>

    <div class="dr-tip">
      <p>Ideally, prehabilitation should begin as soon as surgery is planned. If you&rsquo;re receiving chemotherapy or radiation before surgery, you may have weeks or months to prepare. Use this time wisely.</p>
    </div>

    <h4>Quitting Smoking Before Cancer Surgery</h4>
    <p>If you smoke, quitting before surgery is one of the most important things you can do:</p>
    <ul>
      <li>Smoking increases risk of lung complications, wound infections, blood clots, and longer hospital stays</li>
      <li>Even quitting on the day of surgery has some benefit</li>
      <li>Four weeks of abstinence significantly reduces complications</li>
      <li>Nicotine replacement therapy (patches, gum) is safe before surgery and is NOT a reason to delay</li>
      <li>Quitting should never delay your cancer surgery</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>Medical Optimization</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Diabetes:</strong> Blood sugar control is critical for healing and preventing infection</li>
      <li><strong>Heart/lung disease:</strong> May need evaluation before surgery</li>
      <li><strong>Anemia:</strong> Low blood counts may need correction before surgery</li>
      <li><strong>Dental care:</strong> Important before head/neck cancer surgery or radiation</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>Understanding Your Surgery</h4>

    <p><strong>Surgical Approaches</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Open surgery:</strong> Larger incision for direct access</li>
      <li><strong>Minimally invasive (laparoscopic/robotic):</strong> Smaller incisions, often faster recovery</li>
      <li><strong>Endoscopic:</strong> Through natural openings</li>
    </ul>

    <p><strong>Lymph Node Surgery</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Sentinel node biopsy:</strong> Removing only the first node(s) where cancer would spread</li>
      <li><strong>Lymph node dissection:</strong> Removing a group of lymph nodes</li>
      <li>May cause lymphedema (swelling) &mdash; your team will discuss prevention</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>After Cancer Surgery</h4>

    <p><strong>Enhanced Recovery (ERAS)</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>Early eating and drinking</li>
      <li>Early walking to prevent complications</li>
      <li>Multimodal pain management (not just opioids)</li>
      <li>Early removal of tubes and drains</li>
      <li>Blood clot prevention</li>
    </ul>
    <p>ERAS protocols reduce hospital stays and lower complication rates across many types of cancer surgery.</p>

    <p><strong>Drains and Tubes</strong></p>
    <p>You may go home with surgical drains. Your team will teach you how to empty them, measure output, and recognize problems.</p>

    <p><strong>Pathology Results</strong></p>
    <p>After surgery, tissue is examined by a pathologist (takes several days to a week+). The report determines whether all cancer was removed, the exact type and grade, lymph node involvement, and what additional treatment may be needed.</p>

    <h4>Special Considerations by Cancer Type</h4>

    <p><strong>Breast Cancer</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>Lumpectomy or mastectomy; sentinel node biopsy common</li>
      <li>May go home with drains</li>
      <li>PT can prevent shoulder stiffness and lymphedema</li>
      <li>Discuss reconstruction options before surgery</li>
    </ul>

    <p><strong>Lung Cancer</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>Breathing exercises before surgery are especially important</li>
      <li>Chest tubes after surgery</li>
      <li>Early mobilization is critical</li>
      <li>Pulmonary rehabilitation may be recommended</li>
    </ul>

    <p><strong>Colorectal Cancer</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>May need a temporary or permanent stoma (ostomy)</li>
      <li>If a stoma is planned, you&rsquo;ll meet with a stoma nurse beforehand</li>
      <li>Early eating and walking are especially important</li>
    </ul>

    <p><strong>Head &amp; Neck Cancer</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li>Dental evaluation before surgery is essential</li>
      <li>Speech and swallowing therapy may be needed</li>
      <li>May need a temporary feeding tube</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>Preparing for Additional Treatment</h4>
    <p>Many cancer patients need chemotherapy or radiation after surgery. One of the goals of good surgical recovery is to be well enough to start additional treatment on time. Prehabilitation and ERAS protocols help ensure this.</p>

    <div class="checklist">
      <div class="checklist-title">&#9745; Cancer Surgery Preparation Checklist</div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">I understand my diagnosis and surgical plan</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">I&rsquo;ve asked all my questions</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">I&rsquo;ve started prehabilitation (exercise, nutrition, stress management)</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">If I smoke, I&rsquo;ve started a quit plan</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">Chronic conditions are optimized</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">Medications reviewed with surgical team</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">Preoperative tests completed</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">Home help arranged for recovery</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">Home prepared for recovery</div>
      </div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)">
        <div class="check-box"></div>
        <div class="check-text">Advance directive completed (if desired)</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="dr-tip">
      <p>Preparing well for cancer surgery genuinely improves your outcome. Prehabilitation, quitting smoking, managing distress, and following ERAS protocols all make a measurable difference. Your cancer team is there to support you every step of the way. Don&rsquo;t hesitate to ask questions or express concerns. <a href='https://www.opwellconcierge.com/schedule' style='color:#2D5A3D;font-weight:600;'>Book an OpWell consultation for personalized surgical preparation &rarr;</a></p>
    </div>

    <button class="mark-complete-btn" onclick="markComplete(22)">&#10003; Mark Module Complete</button>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<!-- MODULE 23: COMPLETE L&D PREPARATION HUB          -->
<!-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="module-card bonus" data-module="23" data-relevant-surgery="cesarean">
  <div class="module-header" onclick="toggleModule(23)">
    <div class="module-number">23</div>
    <div class="module-header-text">
      <h3>Your Complete Guide to Labor &amp; Delivery</h3>
      <div class="module-tag bonus-tag">Bonus Module &mdash; Everything expectant mothers need in one place</div>
    </div>
    <div class="module-chevron">&#9662;</div>
  </div>
  <div class="module-body">

    <p>This module brings together everything you need to prepare for labor, delivery, and the early postpartum period &mdash; whether you&rsquo;re planning a vaginal birth or a cesarean section. Think of this as your L&amp;D home base.</p>

    <div class="dr-tip">
      <p>As an anesthesiologist, I&rsquo;ve been in the delivery room thousands of times. The patients who do best are the ones who walk in informed and confident &mdash; not because nothing will surprise them, but because they understand the process and trust their team. That&rsquo;s what this guide is for.</p>
    </div>

    <h4>Preparing for Delivery Day</h4>

    <p><strong>When to Go to the Hospital</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Regular contractions:</strong> 5 minutes apart, lasting 1 minute each, for at least 1 hour (the &ldquo;5-1-1 rule&rdquo;)</li>
      <li><strong>Your water breaks</strong> &mdash; note the time, color, and odor and call your provider</li>
      <li><strong>Heavy vaginal bleeding</strong> (more than spotting)</li>
      <li><strong>Decreased fetal movement</strong> &mdash; if your baby is moving less than usual</li>
      <li><strong>Severe headache, vision changes, or upper abdominal pain</strong> &mdash; could be preeclampsia</li>
      <li><strong>Scheduled C-section:</strong> Arrive at the time your OB team specifies (usually 1&ndash;2 hours before)</li>
    </ul>

    <div class="checklist">
      <div class="checklist-title">&#9745; Hospital Bag Checklist</div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Photo ID and insurance cards</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Birth plan (printed copies for your nurse and anesthesiologist)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Comfortable robe or gown for laboring</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Nursing bra and breast pads</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">High-waisted underwear (won&rsquo;t rub a C-section incision)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Going-home outfit for you (loose, comfortable)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Going-home outfit for baby + swaddle blanket</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Infant car seat installed in the car</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Phone charger (long cord)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Toiletries, lip balm, hair ties</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Snacks for your support person</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Pillow from home (optional but comforting)</div></div>
    </div>

    <h4>Understanding the Stages of Labor</h4>

    <table class="data-table">
      <tr><th>Stage</th><th>What Happens</th><th>How Long</th></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Early labor</strong></td><td>Cervix dilates 0&ndash;6 cm. Contractions are mild to moderate, 5&ndash;30 min apart. You can usually stay home during this phase.</td><td>Hours to days (varies widely)</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Active labor</strong></td><td>Cervix dilates 6&ndash;10 cm. Contractions are strong, regular, 2&ndash;5 min apart. This is when most women go to the hospital and may request an epidural.</td><td>4&ndash;8 hours (first baby); shorter for subsequent</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Pushing &amp; delivery</strong></td><td>Cervix is fully dilated. You push with contractions to deliver your baby.</td><td>Minutes to 2&ndash;3 hours</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Placenta delivery</strong></td><td>The placenta is delivered. Uterine massage and any repairs are done.</td><td>5&ndash;30 minutes</td></tr>
    </table>

    <h4>Your Birth Plan</h4>
    <p>A birth plan communicates your preferences to your care team. Keep it to 1 page and stay flexible &mdash; labor is unpredictable.</p>

    <div class="checklist">
      <div class="checklist-title">&#9745; Birth Plan Decisions to Discuss</div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Pain management preference (epidural, nitrous, unmedicated, open to all options)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Who I want in the delivery room (partner, doula, family member)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Mobility during labor (walking, birthing ball, tub if available)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Immediate skin-to-skin contact after delivery</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Delayed cord clamping preference</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Breastfeeding plan (initiate within first hour)</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">If C-section becomes necessary: clear drape? Skin-to-skin in OR?</div></div>
      <div class="checklist-item" onclick="toggleCheck(this)"><div class="check-box"></div><div class="check-text">Cord blood banking (if planned, have the kit at the hospital)</div></div>
    </div>

    <h4>Anesthesia &amp; Pain Management Options</h4>

    <p>Understanding your pain relief options <em>before</em> labor gives you the power to make informed decisions in the moment. Here&rsquo;s a quick overview &mdash; for the full deep dive, see <strong>Module 20: Pain Management During Labor &amp; Delivery</strong>.</p>

    <table class="data-table">
      <tr><th>Method</th><th>Pain Relief</th><th>Key Facts</th></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Epidural</strong></td><td>Most effective</td><td>Used by 60%+ of US births. Does NOT increase C-section risk. Safe for baby. Can be adjusted throughout labor.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Combined Spinal-Epidural</strong></td><td>Most effective, faster onset</td><td>Rapid relief (minutes) + ongoing epidural. Popular choice.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>IV opioids</strong></td><td>Moderate</td><td>Available early in labor. May cause drowsiness. Usually avoided near delivery.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Nitrous oxide</strong></td><td>Mild-moderate</td><td>You control it. Wears off quickly. Not available everywhere.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Non-medication</strong></td><td>Varies</td><td>Breathing, massage, water therapy, movement, doula support. Can combine with any of the above.</td></tr>
    </table>

    <div class="dr-tip">
      <p>There is no &ldquo;right&rdquo; choice. The best pain management is whatever works for you. Your preferences may change during labor &mdash; and that&rsquo;s completely okay. An epidural at 3 AM after 12 hours of labor is not a failure. It&rsquo;s a tool.</p>
    </div>

    <h4>If You&rsquo;re Having a Planned Cesarean</h4>
    <p>For the full recovery guide, see <strong>Module 19: Cesarean Section Recovery</strong>. Key points:</p>
    <ul>
      <li>You&rsquo;ll likely receive <strong>spinal or epidural anesthesia</strong> &mdash; you&rsquo;ll be awake for the birth</li>
      <li>Your support person can usually be in the OR with you</li>
      <li>Skin-to-skin contact can often happen shortly after delivery</li>
      <li>Surgery takes about 45&ndash;60 minutes; you&rsquo;ll feel pressure but not pain</li>
      <li>Recovery in hospital is typically 2&ndash;3 days</li>
      <li>All recommended pain medications are safe for breastfeeding</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>If an Unplanned Cesarean Becomes Necessary</h4>
    <p>About 1 in 3 US deliveries are cesarean. Sometimes a C-section is needed during labor for:</p>
    <ul>
      <li>Baby showing signs of distress (abnormal heart rate patterns)</li>
      <li>Labor not progressing despite adequate contractions</li>
      <li>Umbilical cord issues</li>
      <li>Placental problems</li>
    </ul>
    <p>If you have an epidural in place, it can usually be used for the C-section &mdash; no general anesthesia needed. If not, a spinal is placed quickly. Your support person can typically stay with you.</p>

    <h4>Breastfeeding Basics</h4>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>First hour:</strong> Skin-to-skin contact and early breastfeeding are encouraged (even after C-section)</li>
      <li><strong>Colostrum</strong> (the first milk) is small in volume but packed with antibodies &mdash; it&rsquo;s all your baby needs at first</li>
      <li>Milk typically &ldquo;comes in&rdquo; 2&ndash;5 days after birth</li>
      <li>Breastfeeding can be uncomfortable at first &mdash; ask for a <strong>lactation consultant</strong> (most hospitals have them)</li>
      <li><strong>C-section positions:</strong> Side-lying or football hold may be more comfortable than cradle hold</li>
      <li>All standard postpartum pain medications (acetaminophen, ibuprofen, most opioids) are safe while breastfeeding</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>Postpartum Recovery</h4>

    <p><strong>What&rsquo;s Normal</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Vaginal bleeding (lochia):</strong> Lasts 4&ndash;6 weeks. Starts heavy, gradually lightens. Use pads only &mdash; no tampons.</li>
      <li><strong>Cramping:</strong> Uterine contractions as your uterus shrinks back. Worse during breastfeeding (oxytocin). Normal.</li>
      <li><strong>Perineal soreness</strong> (vaginal delivery): Ice packs, sitz baths, and witch hazel pads help.</li>
      <li><strong>Incision pain</strong> (C-section): Worst in first 2&ndash;3 days. Take pain medication on schedule.</li>
      <li><strong>Baby blues:</strong> Mood swings, tearfulness, feeling overwhelmed &mdash; affects up to 80% of new mothers. Usually resolves within 2 weeks.</li>
      <li><strong>Fatigue:</strong> Profound. Sleep when baby sleeps. Accept all help offered.</li>
      <li><strong>Constipation:</strong> Very common. Start stool softeners before leaving the hospital.</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>When to Call Your Doctor</h4>
    <ul>
      <li>Fever above 100.4&deg;F (38&deg;C)</li>
      <li>Heavy bleeding (soaking more than 1 pad per hour) or large clots</li>
      <li>Foul-smelling vaginal discharge</li>
      <li>Incision redness, swelling, opening, or drainage (C-section)</li>
      <li>Severe headache that doesn&rsquo;t improve, vision changes, or upper abdominal pain (possible <strong>postpartum preeclampsia</strong> &mdash; can occur up to 6 weeks after delivery)</li>
      <li>Leg swelling, warmth, or pain (possible blood clot)</li>
      <li>Chest pain or difficulty breathing</li>
      <li>Painful, red, warm breast with fever (possible mastitis)</li>
      <li>Difficulty urinating or painful urination</li>
    </ul>

    <h4>Postpartum Mental Health</h4>
    <p><strong>&ldquo;Baby blues&rdquo; vs. postpartum depression:</strong></p>
    <table class="data-table">
      <tr><th>Baby Blues (Normal)</th><th>Postpartum Depression (Seek Help)</th></tr>
      <tr><td>Starts within days of delivery</td><td>Can start anytime in the first year</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Mood swings, tearfulness, irritability</td><td>Persistent sadness, hopelessness, numbness</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Resolves within 2 weeks</td><td>Lasts more than 2 weeks and worsens</td></tr>
      <tr><td>You can still care for yourself and baby</td><td>Difficulty bonding, caring for baby, or functioning</td></tr>
      <tr><td>Comes and goes</td><td>Constant or worsening</td></tr>
    </table>

    <div class="warning-box">
      <div class="warning-title">Important</div>
      <p><strong>Postpartum depression is a medical condition, not a character flaw.</strong> If you experience persistent sadness, inability to care for yourself or your baby, anxiety that won&rsquo;t stop, or thoughts of harming yourself or your baby &mdash; tell someone immediately. Call your OB, go to the ER, or call the Postpartum Support Helpline: <strong>1-800-944-4773</strong>.</p>
    </div>

    <p>OpWell&rsquo;s mental wellness specialist, <strong>Stacey J. Floyd, MA, LPC</strong>, provides perinatal mental health support. <a href="https://www.opwellconcierge.com/schedule" style="color:var(--sage); font-weight:600;">Book a session &rarr;</a></p>

    <h4>Your L&amp;D Preparation Timeline</h4>

    <table class="data-table">
      <tr><th>When</th><th>What to Do</th></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>28&ndash;32 weeks</strong></td><td>Take a childbirth class. Tour the hospital/birth center. Start your birth plan. Begin pelvic floor exercises.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>32&ndash;36 weeks</strong></td><td>Pack your hospital bag. Install the car seat. Discuss anesthesia options with your provider. Consider an OpWell L&amp;D Consultation.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>36&ndash;38 weeks</strong></td><td>Finalize birth plan. Pre-register at the hospital. Stock freezer meals. Arrange help for after delivery.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>38&ndash;40 weeks</strong></td><td>Review when to go to the hospital. Have your bag by the door. Rest as much as possible.</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>After delivery</strong></td><td>Focus on rest, feeding, bonding. Take pain meds on schedule. Accept help. Watch for warning signs.</td></tr>
    </table>

    <h4>Deep-Dive Modules</h4>
    <p>This guide covers the big picture. For detailed deep-dives, see:</p>
    <ul>
      <li><strong>Module 19: Cesarean Section Recovery</strong> &mdash; complete C-section recovery guide (pain management, incision care, breastfeeding, emotional recovery, week-by-week timeline) <a href="#" onclick="toggleModule(19); return false;" style="color:var(--sage); font-weight:600;">Open Module 19 &rarr;</a></li>
      <li><strong>Module 20: Pain Management During Labor &amp; Delivery</strong> &mdash; detailed comparison of all pain relief options (epidural, spinal, CSE, IV opioids, nitrous, non-medication methods) <a href="#" onclick="toggleModule(20); return false;" style="color:var(--sage); font-weight:600;">Open Module 20 &rarr;</a></li>
    </ul>

    <div class="dr-tip">
      <p>Want personalized, one-on-one guidance for your delivery? OpWell offers L&amp;D Consultations where Dr. Oluwole reviews your complete health picture, explains every anesthesia option honestly, addresses your specific fears, and creates a plan so nothing is a surprise on delivery day. <a href="https://www.opwellconcierge.com/schedule" style="color:var(--sage); font-weight:600;">Book your L&amp;D Consultation &rarr;</a></p>
    </div>

    <div class="knowledge-check" data-quiz="23">
      <h4 class="quiz-title">Quick Knowledge Check</h4>
      <div class="quiz-question" data-q="1">
        <p class="quiz-prompt">When should you go to the hospital during labor (the &ldquo;5-1-1 rule&rdquo;)?</p>
        <div class="quiz-options">
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="a" onclick="handleQuiz(this)">Contractions 10 minutes apart for 30 minutes</button>
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="b" data-correct onclick="handleQuiz(this)">Contractions 5 minutes apart, lasting 1 minute, for 1 hour</button>
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="c" onclick="handleQuiz(this)">As soon as you feel any contractions</button>
        </div>
        <div class="quiz-feedback"></div>
      </div>
      <div class="quiz-question" data-q="2">
        <p class="quiz-prompt">True or False: Getting an epidural increases your chance of needing a cesarean section.</p>
        <div class="quiz-options">
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="a" onclick="handleQuiz(this)">True</button>
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="b" data-correct onclick="handleQuiz(this)">False &mdash; epidurals do NOT increase cesarean risk</button>
        </div>
        <div class="quiz-feedback"></div>
      </div>
      <div class="quiz-question" data-q="3">
        <p class="quiz-prompt">&ldquo;Baby blues&rdquo; typically resolve within:</p>
        <div class="quiz-options">
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="a" data-correct onclick="handleQuiz(this)">2 weeks</button>
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="b" onclick="handleQuiz(this)">6 months</button>
          <button class="quiz-opt" data-answer="c" onclick="handleQuiz(this)">They don&rsquo;t resolve without treatment</button>
        </div>
        <div class="quiz-feedback"></div>
      </div>

<script>
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// STATE
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
let selectedSurgery = null;
let completedModules = new Set();
let TOTAL_MODULES = 23;
let intakeData = null;
let intakeStep = 1;
const INTAKE_STEPS = 8;
let quizAnswers = {};

// Surgery labels
const SURGERY_LABELS = {
  cosmetic:'Cosmetic Surgery', bariatric:'Bariatric Surgery', general:'General Surgery',
  orthopedic:'Orthopedic Surgery', gynecologic:'Gynecologic Surgery', cesarean:'C-Section',
  cancer:'Cancer Surgery', notsure:'Comprehensive View (All Types)'
};

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// INTAKE WIZARD
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function buildIntakeDots() {
  const c = document.getElementById('intakeDots');
  c.innerHTML = '';
  for (let i = 1; i <= INTAKE_STEPS; i++) {
    const d = document.createElement('div');
    d.className = 'intake-dot' + (i === 1 ? ' active' : '');
    d.textContent = i;
    c.appendChild(d);
  }
}

function showIntakeStep(n) {
  intakeStep = n;
  document.querySelectorAll('.intake-step').forEach(s => s.classList.remove('active'));
  const step = document.querySelector(`.intake-step[data-step="${n}"]`);
  if (step) step.classList.add('active');

  // Update dots
  document.querySelectorAll('.intake-dot').forEach((d, i) => {
    d.classList.toggle('active', i + 1 === n);
    d.classList.toggle('completed', i + 1 < n);
  });

  // Show/hide nav
  const nav = document.getElementById('intakeNav');
  if (n > 1) {
    nav.style.display = 'flex';
    nav.querySelector('.intake-back').style.visibility = n > 1 ? 'visible' : 'hidden';
  } else {
    nav.style.display = 'none';
  }

  // Next button state — check if current step has selection
  updateNextBtn();
}

function updateNextBtn() {
  const step = document.querySelector(`.intake-step[data-step="${intakeStep}"]`);
  const btn = document.querySelector('.intake-next');
  if (!step || !btn) return;

  const hasSelection = step.querySelector('.intake-opt.selected');
  btn.disabled = !hasSelection;

  // Change text on last step
  if (intakeStep === INTAKE_STEPS) {
    btn.innerHTML = 'See My Personalized Guide &#8594;';
  } else {
    btn.innerHTML = 'Next &#8594;';
  }
}

function pickIntake(el) {
  // Single-select: deselect siblings
  el.closest('.intake-options').querySelectorAll('.intake-opt').forEach(o => o.classList.remove('selected'));
  el.classList.add('selected');
  updateNextBtn();

  // Auto-advance after brief confirmation delay for single-select steps
  setTimeout(() => intakeNext(), 500);
}

function pickMulti(el) {
  // Multi-select toggle
  const noneOpt = el.closest('.intake-options').querySelector('[data-value="none"]');
  if (el.dataset.value === 'none') {
    pickNone(el);
    return;
  }
  el.classList.toggle('selected');
  // Deselect "none" if any condition is selected
  if (noneOpt) noneOpt.classList.remove('selected');
  updateNextBtn();
}

function pickNone(el) {
  // Deselect all others, select "none", auto-advance
  el.closest('.intake-options').querySelectorAll('.intake-opt').forEach(o => o.classList.remove('selected'));
  el.classList.add('selected');
  updateNextBtn();
  setTimeout(() => intakeNext(), 500);
}

function intakeNext() {
  if (intakeStep >= INTAKE_STEPS) {
    completeIntake();
    return;
  }

  // Validate current step has selection
  const step = document.querySelector(`.intake-step[data-step="${intakeStep}"]`);
  if (!step.querySelector('.intake-opt.selected')) return;

  showIntakeStep(intakeStep + 1);
}

function intakeBack() {
  if (intakeStep > 1) showIntakeStep(intakeStep - 1);
}

function collectIntakeData() {
  const data = {};
  // Step 1: surgery
  const s1 = document.querySelector('.intake-step[data-step="1"] .intake-opt.selected');
  data.surgery = s1 ? s1.dataset.value : 'notsure';
  // Step 2: timeline
  const s2 = document.querySelector('.intake-step[data-step="2"] .intake-opt.selected');
  data.timeline = s2 ? s2.dataset.value : '2-4weeks';
  // Step 3: conditions (multi)
  data.conditions = [];
  document.querySelectorAll('.intake-step[data-step="3"] .intake-opt.selected').forEach(o => {
    if (o.dataset.value !== 'none') data.conditions.push(o.dataset.value);
  });
  // Step 4: smoking
  const s4 = document.querySelector('.intake-step[data-step="4"] .intake-opt.selected');
  data.smoking = s4 ? s4.dataset.value : 'no';
  // Step 5: first surgery
  const s5 = document.querySelector('.intake-step[data-step="5"] .intake-opt.selected');
  data.firstSurgery = s5 ? s5.dataset.value === 'first' : false;
  // Step 6: anxiety
  const s6 = document.querySelector('.intake-step[data-step="6"] .intake-opt.selected');
  data.anxiety = s6 ? s6.dataset.value : 'low';
  // Step 7: caregiver
  const s7 = document.querySelector('.intake-step[data-step="7"] .intake-opt.selected');
  data.caregiver = s7 ? s7.dataset.value : 'no';
  // Step 8: activity
  const s8 = document.querySelector('.intake-step[data-step="8"] .intake-opt.selected');
  data.activity = s8 ? s8.dataset.value : 'moderate';

  return data;
}

function completeIntake() {
  intakeData = collectIntakeData();
  localStorage.setItem('mc_intake', JSON.stringify(intakeData));

  // Hide wizard, show compact profile
  document.getElementById('intakeSection').style.display = 'none';
  applyPersonalization(intakeData);
  renderProfileSummary(intakeData);
  document.getElementById('profileSummary').style.display = 'block';
  document.getElementById('readinessSection').style.display = 'flex';
}

function retakeIntake() {
  localStorage.removeItem('mc_intake');
  intakeData = null;
  // Reset selections
  document.querySelectorAll('.intake-opt').forEach(o => o.classList.remove('selected'));
  // Reset all condition-based content
  document.querySelectorAll('[data-condition]').forEach(el => el.classList.remove('condition-show'));
  document.querySelectorAll('.risk-callout-injected').forEach(el => el.remove());
  document.querySelector('.main-container').classList.remove('timeline-postop','timeline-urgent');
  // Show wizard
  document.getElementById('intakeSection').style.display = 'block';
  document.getElementById('profileSummary').style.display = 'none';
  showIntakeStep(1);
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// PERSONALIZATION ENGINE
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function generateTags(data) {
  const tags = [];
  // Timeline
  if (data.timeline === '<1week') tags.push('urgent');
  if (data.timeline === '1-2weeks') tags.push('compressed');
  if (data.timeline === 'postop') tags.push('postop');
  if (data.timeline === '4+weeks') tags.push('prehab-priority');
  // Conditions
  data.conditions.forEach(c => tags.push('risk-' + c));
  // Smoking
  if (data.smoking === 'yes') tags.push('smoker');
  if (data.smoking === 'quit-recent') tags.push('recent-quit');
  // First surgery
  if (data.firstSurgery) tags.push('first-timer');
  // Anxiety
  if (data.anxiety === 'high') tags.push('high-anxiety');
  if (data.anxiety === 'moderate') tags.push('moderate-anxiety');
  // Caregiver
  if (data.caregiver === 'no') tags.push('no-caregiver');
  if (data.caregiver === 'yes-involved') tags.push('has-caregiver');
  // Activity
  if (data.activity === 'sedentary') tags.push('sedentary');
  // Elevated risk
  if (data.conditions.some(c => ['diabetes','heart','lung','obesity','osa','clots'].includes(c)) || data.smoking === 'yes') {
    tags.push('elevated-risk');
  }
  return tags;
}

function applyPersonalization(data) {
  const tags = generateTags(data);

  // 1. Surgery filtering (existing system)
  selectSurgery(data.surgery, true);

  // 2. Conditional content blocks
  document.querySelectorAll('[data-condition]').forEach(el => {
    const cond = el.dataset.condition;
    let show = false;
    if (cond.startsWith('!')) {
      show = !tags.includes(cond.substring(1));
    } else {
      show = tags.includes(cond);
    }
    el.classList.toggle('condition-show', show);
  });

  // 3. Risk callouts (injected dynamically)
  injectRiskCallouts(tags, data);

  // 4. Timeline modes
  const mc = document.querySelector('.main-container');
  mc.classList.remove('timeline-postop', 'timeline-urgent');
  if (data.timeline === 'postop') mc.classList.add('timeline-postop');
  if (data.timeline === '<1week') mc.classList.add('timeline-urgent');

  // 5. Caregiver module visibility
  const caregiverModule = document.querySelector('[data-module="14"]');
  if (caregiverModule) {
    caregiverModule.classList.remove('surgery-hidden', 'surgery-highlighted');
    if (data.caregiver === 'no') {
      caregiverModule.classList.add('surgery-hidden');
    } else if (data.caregiver === 'yes-involved' || data.caregiver === 'yes-limited') {
      caregiverModule.classList.add('surgery-highlighted');
    }
  }

  // 6. Update visible module count for progress
  updateVisibleModuleCount();

  saveState();
}

function injectRiskCallouts(tags, data) {
  // Remove previously injected
  document.querySelectorAll('.risk-callout-injected').forEach(el => el.remove());

  const callouts = [];

  if (tags.includes('risk-diabetes')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 1, html: '<p><strong>Because you have diabetes:</strong> Blood sugar control is critical before surgery. Poorly controlled diabetes increases infection risk and slows wound healing. Key medication guidance:</p><ul style="margin:8px 0 0 16px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;"><li><strong>Metformin:</strong> Hold the day of surgery. Restart when eating normally.</li><li><strong>SGLT2 inhibitors</strong> (Jardiance, Farxiga, Invokana): <strong>Stop 3&ndash;4 days before surgery</strong> &mdash; risk of diabetic ketoacidosis under anesthesia.</li><li><strong>Insulin:</strong> Your doctor will adjust your dose &mdash; do NOT take your full morning dose while fasting. Typically take 50&ndash;80% of long-acting insulin the night before.</li><li><strong>Sulfonylureas</strong> (glipizide, glyburide): Hold the morning of surgery (risk of low blood sugar).</li><li><strong>A1C goal:</strong> Below 8% before elective surgery (ideally below 7%).</li></ul>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 2, html: '<p><strong>Diabetes &amp; nutrition:</strong> Focus on stable blood sugar in the weeks before surgery. Avoid processed carbs and sugar spikes. Eat consistent meals with protein and fiber. If your doctor recommends carbohydrate loading the night before surgery, discuss whether this is appropriate for your diabetes.</p>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 6, html: '<p><strong>Diabetes &amp; recovery:</strong> Blood sugar often runs higher after surgery due to surgical stress, pain, and medications. Key recovery points:</p><ul style="margin:8px 0 0 16px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;"><li>Check blood sugar more frequently (every 4&ndash;6 hours)</li><li>Target: 140&ndash;180 mg/dL during recovery (your team may give a tighter range)</li><li>Report blood sugar consistently above 250 mg/dL</li><li>Watch wounds closely &mdash; high blood sugar slows healing and increases infection risk</li><li>Resume diabetes medications as directed &mdash; do NOT restart SGLT2 inhibitors until eating and drinking normally</li></ul>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('smoker') || tags.includes('recent-quit')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 3, html: '<p><strong>' + (tags.includes('smoker') ? 'You smoke:' : 'You recently quit:') + '</strong> ' + (tags.includes('smoker') ? 'Quitting before surgery is one of the most impactful things you can do. Even 4 weeks of abstinence significantly reduces lung complications, wound infections, and blood clot risk. Nicotine replacement (patches, gum) is safe and is NOT a reason to delay surgery. Ask your doctor for help.' : 'Great decision. Continue staying smoke-free through surgery and recovery. Your lungs and wound healing will benefit significantly.') + '</p>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 6, html: '<p><strong>Wound healing:</strong> Smoking impairs blood flow to healing tissues. ' + (tags.includes('smoker') ? 'If you can quit even now, your wound will heal better.' : 'Staying smoke-free will help your incision heal properly.') + '</p>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('risk-osa')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 5, html: '<p><strong>Sleep apnea &mdash; important for your safety:</strong></p><ul style="margin:8px 0 0 16px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;"><li><strong>Bring your CPAP/BiPAP machine</strong>, mask, headgear, and power cord to the hospital</li><li>Tell your anesthesiologist about your sleep apnea &mdash; it affects airway management and monitoring</li><li>Share your pressure settings and bring your sleep study results if available</li><li>Continue using CPAP every night leading up to surgery &mdash; good compliance improves outcomes</li><li>After surgery: you will use CPAP in recovery, may have continuous oxygen monitoring, and your pain management will be carefully planned to minimize opioid-related breathing depression</li><li><strong>Avoid sleeping flat on your back</strong> after surgery &mdash; keep the head of bed elevated</li></ul>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('risk-obesity')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 1, html: '<p><strong>Weight &amp; surgical preparation:</strong></p><ul style="margin:8px 0 0 16px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;"><li>Antibiotic doses will be adjusted for your weight to prevent infection</li><li><strong>Blood clot prevention is critical</strong> &mdash; you are at higher risk for DVT/PE. You will receive compression devices and possibly blood thinners.</li><li>Even 5&ndash;10% weight loss before surgery can meaningfully reduce risk (if time allows)</li><li>Prehabilitation (Module 3) is especially beneficial &mdash; start a walking program now</li><li>If you snore or have been told you stop breathing at night, ask about screening for sleep apnea before surgery</li></ul>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 6, html: '<p><strong>Recovery priorities:</strong> Early and frequent walking is critical for preventing blood clots and lung complications. Deep breathing exercises every 1&ndash;2 hours. Keep head of bed elevated. Report any leg swelling, chest pain, or shortness of breath immediately.</p>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('risk-clots')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 1, html: '<p><strong>Blood thinner management:</strong> Because you have a history of blood clots or take blood thinners, careful coordination is essential:</p><ul style="margin:8px 0 0 16px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;"><li><strong>Warfarin:</strong> Typically stop 5 days before surgery. INR checked before procedure. May need bridging with injectable blood thinner.</li><li><strong>Eliquis/Xarelto/Edoxaban:</strong> Stop 2&ndash;3 days before (high bleeding risk surgery) or 1&ndash;2 days (low risk).</li><li><strong>Plavix:</strong> Stop 5 days before. <strong>Brilinta:</strong> Stop 5 days. <strong>Effient:</strong> Stop 7 days.</li><li><strong>Aspirin:</strong> Often continued &mdash; discuss with your surgeon.</li><li>NEVER stop blood thinners without instructions from both your prescribing doctor AND your surgeon.</li><li>Know when to restart after surgery &mdash; your team will give specific timing.</li></ul>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 7, html: '<p><strong>Blood clot history &mdash; extra vigilance:</strong> You are at higher risk for recurrent DVT/PE. Your surgeon should have a specific prevention plan (compression devices + blood thinners). Know the DVT and PE warning signs in this module &mdash; they are especially important for you. After surgery, walk as soon and as often as allowed. Do ankle pumps every hour. Stay hydrated. Take blood thinners exactly as prescribed &mdash; do not skip doses.</p>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('risk-heart')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 1, html: '<p><strong>Heart disease &mdash; medication guidance:</strong></p><ul style="margin:8px 0 0 16px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;"><li><strong>Beta-blockers</strong> (metoprolol, carvedilol, atenolol): <strong>Continue</strong> &mdash; do NOT stop suddenly. Take with a sip of water morning of surgery.</li><li><strong>Statins</strong> (atorvastatin, rosuvastatin): <strong>Continue</strong> through surgery.</li><li><strong>ACE inhibitors</strong> (-pril meds) and <strong>ARBs</strong> (-sartan meds): Often held the morning of surgery &mdash; your team will advise.</li><li><strong>Diuretics</strong> (water pills): Often held morning of surgery to prevent dehydration.</li><li><strong>If you have a coronary stent:</strong> Timing of surgery matters. Bare metal stent: wait 30+ days. Drug-eluting stent: wait 3&ndash;12 months. NEVER stop antiplatelet medications without your cardiologist&rsquo;s approval.</li><li>If you have a pacemaker or defibrillator, bring your cardiac device card.</li></ul>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 5, html: '<p><strong>Heart disease &mdash; surgery day:</strong> Your anesthesiologist needs to know about your heart condition, all cardiac medications, and recent tests (EKG, echocardiogram, stress test). Bring this information. Quick self-test: can you climb 2 flights of stairs without stopping? If not, additional cardiac evaluation may be needed before surgery.</p>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 7, html: '<p><strong>Cardiac warning signs after surgery:</strong> Contact your team immediately for chest pain or pressure, shortness of breath, irregular or racing heartbeat, unusual fatigue or weakness, new swelling in legs or feet, or dizziness/fainting. These may indicate a cardiac complication that needs urgent attention.</p>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('risk-lung')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 3, html: '<p><strong>Lung disease &mdash; prehabilitation is critical for you:</strong></p><ul style="margin:8px 0 0 16px;font-size:14px;line-height:1.7;"><li>Practice incentive spirometer 10 times every hour while awake &mdash; start NOW, not after surgery</li><li>Diaphragmatic breathing exercises daily (see technique in this module)</li><li><strong>Bring all inhalers</strong> to the hospital &mdash; use your rescue inhaler morning of surgery if instructed</li><li>If on inhaled steroids, continue them through surgery</li><li>If you have COPD: walking and breathing exercises for 4+ weeks before surgery can significantly reduce lung complications</li><li><strong>If you smoke:</strong> Quitting even 4 weeks before surgery measurably reduces pulmonary complications</li></ul>' });
    callouts.push({ module: 6, html: '<p><strong>Lung disease &mdash; recovery priorities:</strong> Deep breathing exercises and incentive spirometer every 1&ndash;2 hours while awake. Sit upright as much as possible. Walk early and often &mdash; this is the single best thing for your lungs. Cough and clear secretions (splint your incision with a pillow). Report any increasing shortness of breath, persistent cough, or fever to your team.</p>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('first-timer') && tags.includes('high-anxiety')) {
    // Combined callout to avoid stacking two in the same module
    callouts.push({ module: 4, html: '<p><strong>First surgery + high anxiety:</strong> Feeling very anxious before your first surgery is completely normal &mdash; over 80% of first-time patients feel the same way. The mental preparation techniques in this module are especially valuable for you. Take your time with the visualization exercise, and consider speaking with OpWell&rsquo;s mental wellness specialist, Stacey J. Floyd, MA, LPC, for personalized support. <a href="https://www.opwellconcierge.com/schedule" style="color:var(--sage);font-weight:600;">Book a session &rarr;</a></p>' });
  } else if (tags.includes('first-timer')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 4, html: '<p><strong>First surgery?</strong> Feeling anxious is completely normal. Over 80% of first-time surgical patients feel significant anxiety. The mental preparation techniques in this module are especially valuable for you &mdash; take your time with the visualization exercise.</p>' });
  } else if (tags.includes('high-anxiety')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 4, html: '<p><strong>High anxiety noted:</strong> We hear you, and your feelings are valid. This module has evidence-based techniques that genuinely help. Consider speaking with OpWell&rsquo;s mental wellness specialist, Stacey J. Floyd, MA, LPC, for personalized support. <a href="https://www.opwellconcierge.com/schedule" style="color:var(--sage);font-weight:600;">Book a session &rarr;</a></p>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('sedentary')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 3, html: '<p><strong>Starting from sedentary:</strong> That&rsquo;s completely okay. Start with 10&ndash;15 minute walks and build up gradually. Focus on the Beginner column in the weekly prehab schedule below. Even small amounts of movement make a meaningful difference in your surgical outcome.</p>' });
  }

  if (tags.includes('no-caregiver')) {
    callouts.push({ module: 6, html: '<p><strong>Recovering solo:</strong> Since you&rsquo;ll be mostly on your own, preparation is even more important. Before surgery: stock your home with easy meals, set up a recovery station, fill prescriptions, arrange a ride home. Consider hiring a home health aide for the first 48&ndash;72 hours if possible.</p>' });
  }

  if (data.caregiver === 'yes-limited') {
    callouts.push({ module: 6, html: '<p><strong>Limited caregiver help:</strong> Since your caregiver&rsquo;s availability is limited, plan ahead. Prepare meals in advance, set medication alarms on your phone, and ask your caregiver to focus on the first 24&ndash;48 hours when you&rsquo;ll need the most help. Check out Module 14 (Caregiver&rsquo;s Guide) &mdash; share it with your helper so they know exactly what to prioritize.</p>' });
  }

  // Inject callouts
  callouts.forEach(c => {
    const moduleBody = document.querySelector(`[data-module="${c.module}"] .module-body`);
    if (!moduleBody) return;
    const div = document.createElement('div');
    div.className = 'risk-callout risk-callout-injected';
    div.innerHTML = c.html;
    // Insert after the first paragraph
    const firstP = moduleBody.querySelector('p');
    if (firstP && firstP.nextSibling) {
      firstP.parentNode.insertBefore(div, firstP.nextSibling);
    } else {
      moduleBody.prepend(div);
    }
  });
}

function renderProfileSummary(data) {
  const tags = generateTags(data);
  const tagsEl = document.getElementById('profileTags');
  const msgEl = document.getElementById('profileMessage');
  tagsEl.innerHTML = '';

  // Surgery type tag
  addTag(tagsEl, SURGERY_LABELS[data.surgery] || data.surgery, 'info');

  // Timeline tag
  const timeLabels = {'<1week':'Less than 1 week','1-2weeks':'1-2 weeks','2-4weeks':'2-4 weeks','4+weeks':'4+ weeks','postop':'Post-surgery'};
  addTag(tagsEl, timeLabels[data.timeline] || data.timeline, data.timeline === '<1week' ? 'warning' : 'neutral');

  // Conditions
  const condLabels = {diabetes:'Diabetes',heart:'Heart Disease',lung:'Lung Disease',obesity:'Obesity',osa:'Sleep Apnea',anxiety:'Anxiety/Depression',clots:'Blood Clot History',pain:'Chronic Pain'};
  data.conditions.forEach(c => addTag(tagsEl, condLabels[c] || c, 'warning'));

  // Smoking
  if (data.smoking === 'yes') addTag(tagsEl, 'Current Smoker', 'warning');
  if (data.smoking === 'quit-recent') addTag(tagsEl, 'Recently Quit Smoking', 'neutral');

  // First timer
  if (data.firstSurgery) addTag(tagsEl, 'First Surgery', 'info');

  // Anxiety
  if (data.anxiety === 'high') addTag(tagsEl, 'High Anxiety', 'warning');
  if (data.anxiety === 'moderate') addTag(tagsEl, 'Moderate Anxiety', 'neutral');

  // Caregiver
  if (data.caregiver === 'yes-involved') addTag(tagsEl, 'Caregiver Support', 'info');
  if (data.caregiver === 'no') addTag(tagsEl, 'Solo Recovery', 'neutral');

  // Activity
  const actLabels = {sedentary:'Sedentary',light:'Light Activity',moderate:'Moderate Activity',active:'Very Active'};
  addTag(tagsEl, actLabels[data.activity] || data.activity, data.activity === 'sedentary' ? 'warning' : 'neutral');

  // Message
  let msg = 'Your guide has been personalized based on your profile. ';
  if (data.timeline === 'postop') msg += 'Since you\'ve already had surgery, pre-operative modules are dimmed and recovery content is prioritized. ';
  else if (data.timeline === '<1week') msg += 'With surgery less than a week away, we\'ve focused on the essentials. ';
  if (tags.includes('elevated-risk')) msg += 'Important safety callouts have been added throughout your modules based on your health conditions. ';
  if (data.firstSurgery) msg += 'As a first-time surgical patient, you\'ll see extra context to help you know what to expect. ';
  if (data.anxiety === 'high') msg += 'Mental preparation content has been highlighted for you. ';
  msgEl.textContent = msg;
}

function addTag(container, text, type) {
  const t = document.createElement('span');
  t.className = 'profile-tag ' + type;
  t.textContent = text;
  container.appendChild(t);
}

function updateVisibleModuleCount() {
  const visible = document.querySelectorAll('.module-card:not(.surgery-hidden):not([style*="display: none"])');
  TOTAL_MODULES = visible.length;
  updateProgress();
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// SURGERY SELECTOR (existing, preserved)
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function selectSurgery(type, skipSave) {
  selectedSurgery = type;

  // Show/hide specialty content within modules
  document.querySelectorAll('[data-surgery]').forEach(el => {
    if (type === 'notsure') {
      el.classList.add('show');
    } else {
      el.classList.toggle('show', el.dataset.surgery === type);
    }
  });

  // Show/hide surgery-specific bonus modules
  document.querySelectorAll('[data-relevant-surgery]').forEach(el => {
    if (type === 'notsure') {
      el.classList.remove('surgery-hidden');
      el.classList.remove('surgery-highlighted');
    } else if (el.dataset.relevantSurgery === type) {
      el.classList.remove('surgery-hidden');
      el.classList.add('surgery-highlighted');
    } else {
      el.classList.add('surgery-hidden');
      el.classList.remove('surgery-highlighted');
    }
  });

  // Rebuild progress dots to reflect visible modules
  buildProgressDots();
  updateProgress();

  if (!skipSave) saveState();
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// MODULE TOGGLE
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function toggleModule(num) {
  const card = document.querySelector(`[data-module="${num}"]`);
  if (!card) return;

  const wasOpen = card.classList.contains('open');
  if (wasOpen) {
    card.classList.remove('open');
  } else {
    card.classList.add('open');
    setTimeout(() => {
      card.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
    }, 100);
  }
  updateProgressDots();
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// MARK COMPLETE
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function markComplete(num) {
  if (completedModules.has(num)) {
    completedModules.delete(num);
    document.querySelector(`[data-module="${num}"]`).classList.remove('completed');
  } else {
    completedModules.add(num);
    document.querySelector(`[data-module="${num}"]`).classList.add('completed');
  }
  updateProgress();
  saveState();
  checkCertificateEligibility();
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// COMPLETION CERTIFICATE
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function checkCertificateEligibility() {
  const coreModules = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7];
  const allCoreComplete = coreModules.every(m => completedModules.has(m));

  // Calculate readiness score
  const quizKeys = Object.keys(quizAnswers);
  const correct = quizKeys.filter(k => quizAnswers[k].correct).length;
  const total = quizKeys.length;
  const readinessScore = total > 0 ? Math.round((correct / total) * 100) : 0;

  if (allCoreComplete && readinessScore >= 70) {
    showCertificate(readinessScore);
  }
}

function showCertificate(readinessScore) {
  // Don't show if already shown this session
  if (document.getElementById('certificateModal')) return;

  const surgeryLabel = intakeData ? (SURGERY_LABELS[intakeData.surgery] || 'General Surgery') : 'General Surgery';
  const patientName = intakeData ? (localStorage.getItem('mc_patient_name') || 'Patient') : 'Patient';
  const today = new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'long', day: 'numeric' });
  const completedCount = completedModules.size;

  const modal = document.createElement('div');
  modal.id = 'certificateModal';
  modal.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;inset:0;background:rgba(0,0,0,0.7);z-index:9999;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:20px;';
  modal.innerHTML = `
    <div id="certificateCard" style="background:#fff;border-radius:16px;max-width:600px;width:100%;max-height:90vh;overflow-y:auto;box-shadow:0 20px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);">
      <div style="background:linear-gradient(135deg,#1C2B22,#2D5A3D);padding:2rem 2rem 1.5rem;text-align:center;border-radius:16px 16px 0 0;">
        <div style="font-size:0.65rem;letter-spacing:0.15em;text-transform:uppercase;color:rgba(250,240,232,0.6);margin-bottom:0.5rem;">OpWell Concierge&trade;</div>
        <div style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif;font-size:1.5rem;color:#FAF0E8;font-weight:700;">Certificate of Completion</div>
        <div style="width:50px;height:2px;background:#C8845A;margin:0.75rem auto;"></div>
        <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:rgba(250,240,232,0.7);">POQI-Aligned Surgery Preparation Program</div>
      </div>
      <div style="padding:2rem;text-align:center;">
        <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#888;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.1em;margin-bottom:0.25rem;">This certifies that</div>
        <div style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif;font-size:1.3rem;color:#1C1C1C;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:1rem;border-bottom:1px solid #e0d8d0;padding-bottom:0.75rem;" contenteditable="true" id="certName">${patientName}</div>
        <div style="font-size:0.82rem;color:#555;line-height:1.7;margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
          has successfully completed the <strong>Complete Surgery Preparation Masterclass</strong>
          — a 23-module evidence-based perioperative education program
          personalized for <strong>${surgeryLabel}</strong>.
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex;justify-content:center;gap:2rem;margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
          <div style="text-align:center;">
            <div style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif;font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;color:#2D5A3D;">${completedCount}</div>
            <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:#888;">Modules Completed</div>
          </div>
          <div style="text-align:center;">
            <div style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif;font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;color:#C8845A;">${readinessScore}%</div>
            <div style="font-size:0.7rem;color:#888;">Readiness Score</div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:#888;margin-bottom:0.5rem;">${today}</div>
        <div style="font-size:0.82rem;color:#2D5A3D;font-weight:600;">Dr. Ornella Oluwole, MD, D.ABA</div>
        <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#888;">Board-Certified Anesthesiologist &bull; OpWell Concierge&trade;</div>
        <div style="margin-top:1.25rem;padding-top:1rem;border-top:1px solid #e0d8d0;">
          <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#aaa;line-height:1.5;">This certificate confirms completion of an educational program and does not constitute medical clearance.<br>Based on ERAS protocols, ASA guidelines, and POQI consensus recommendations.</div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div style="padding:0 2rem 1.5rem;display:flex;gap:10px;justify-content:center;">
        <button onclick="printCertificate()" style="padding:10px 24px;background:#2D5A3D;color:#fff;border:none;border-radius:8px;font-family:'DM Sans',sans-serif;font-weight:600;cursor:pointer;font-size:0.88rem;">Print Certificate</button>
        <button onclick="document.getElementById('certificateModal').remove()" style="padding:10px 24px;background:#f0ebe5;color:#555;border:none;border-radius:8px;font-family:'DM Sans',sans-serif;font-weight:600;cursor:pointer;font-size:0.88rem;">Close</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  `;
  document.body.appendChild(modal);
  modal.addEventListener('click', function(e) { if (e.target === modal) modal.remove(); });
}

function printCertificate() {
  const cert = document.getElementById('certificateCard');
  const win = window.open('', '_blank');
  win.document.write('<html><head><title>OpWell Certificate</title>');
  win.document.write('<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:wght@400;700&family=DM+Sans:wght@400;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">');
  win.document.write('<style>body{margin:0;padding:40px;font-family:"DM Sans",sans-serif;} @media print{body{padding:0.5in;}}</style>');
  win.document.write('</head><body>');
  win.document.write(cert.outerHTML);
  win.document.write('</body></html>');
  win.document.close();
  setTimeout(() => win.print(), 500);
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// CHECKLIST
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function toggleCheck(item) {
  item.classList.toggle('checked');
  saveState();
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// PROGRESS
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function buildProgressDots() {
  const container = document.getElementById('progressDots');
  container.innerHTML = '';
  const visibleModules = document.querySelectorAll('.module-card:not(.surgery-hidden)');
  let dotIndex = 1;
  visibleModules.forEach(card => {
    const moduleNum = parseInt(card.dataset.module);
    const dot = document.createElement('div');
    dot.className = 'progress-dot';
    dot.dataset.moduleNum = moduleNum;
    dot.textContent = dotIndex;
    dot.title = 'Module ' + dotIndex;
    dot.onclick = function() { toggleModule(moduleNum); };
    if (completedModules.has(moduleNum)) dot.classList.add('completed');
    container.appendChild(dot);
    dotIndex++;
  });
}

function updateProgress() {
  const visibleModules = document.querySelectorAll('.module-card:not(.surgery-hidden)');
  const visibleCount = visibleModules.length;
  const completedVisible = Array.from(visibleModules).filter(m => m.classList.contains('completed')).length;
  const pct = visibleCount > 0 ? Math.round((completedVisible / visibleCount) * 100) : 0;
  document.getElementById('progressFill').style.width = pct + '%';
  document.getElementById('progressPct').textContent = completedVisible + ' of ' + visibleCount + ' complete';
  updateProgressDots();
}

function updateProgressDots() {
  const dots = document.querySelectorAll('.progress-dot');
  const openModule = document.querySelector('.module-card.open');
  const openNum = openModule ? parseInt(openModule.dataset.module) : null;

  dots.forEach(dot => {
    const num = parseInt(dot.dataset.moduleNum);
    dot.classList.toggle('completed', completedModules.has(num));
    dot.classList.toggle('active', num === openNum && !completedModules.has(num));
  });
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// KNOWLEDGE CHECK QUIZZES
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function handleQuiz(btn) {
  const question = btn.closest('.quiz-question');
  const quiz = btn.closest('.knowledge-check');
  if (!question || !quiz) return;

  const qKey = quiz.dataset.quiz + '-' + question.dataset.q;
  if (quizAnswers[qKey]) return; // Already answered

  const isCorrect = btn.hasAttribute('data-correct');
  quizAnswers[qKey] = { answer: btn.dataset.answer, correct: isCorrect };

  // Disable all options
  question.querySelectorAll('.quiz-opt').forEach(o => {
    o.disabled = true;
    if (o.hasAttribute('data-correct')) o.classList.add('correct');
  });
  if (!isCorrect) btn.classList.add('wrong');

  // Show feedback
  const fb = question.querySelector('.quiz-feedback');
  if (fb) {
    fb.classList.add('show', isCorrect ? 'correct' : 'wrong');
    fb.textContent = isCorrect ? 'Correct!' : 'Not quite. The correct answer is highlighted above.';
  }

  // Save and update score
  localStorage.setItem('mc_quizzes', JSON.stringify(quizAnswers));
  updateReadinessScore();
}

function updateReadinessScore() {
  const keys = Object.keys(quizAnswers);
  if (keys.length === 0) return;

  const correct = keys.filter(k => quizAnswers[k].correct).length;
  const total = keys.length;
  const pct = Math.round((correct / total) * 100);

  document.getElementById('readinessNum').textContent = pct + '%';
  document.getElementById('readinessRing').style.setProperty('--pct', pct + '%');
  document.getElementById('readinessLabel').textContent = correct + ' of ' + total + ' correct — ' + (pct >= 80 ? 'You\'re well prepared!' : pct >= 50 ? 'Good progress! Review missed questions.' : 'Keep learning — revisit modules you missed.');
}

function loadQuizState() {
  try {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem('mc_quizzes');
    if (saved) {
      quizAnswers = JSON.parse(saved);
      // Restore quiz UI
      Object.keys(quizAnswers).forEach(key => {
        const [quizNum, qNum] = key.split('-');
        const quiz = document.querySelector(`.knowledge-check[data-quiz="${quizNum}"]`);
        if (!quiz) return;
        const question = quiz.querySelector(`.quiz-question[data-q="${qNum}"]`);
        if (!question) return;
        question.querySelectorAll('.quiz-opt').forEach(o => {
          o.disabled = true;
          if (o.hasAttribute('data-correct')) o.classList.add('correct');
          if (o.dataset.answer === quizAnswers[key].answer && !quizAnswers[key].correct) o.classList.add('wrong');
        });
        const fb = question.querySelector('.quiz-feedback');
        if (fb) {
          fb.classList.add('show', quizAnswers[key].correct ? 'correct' : 'wrong');
          fb.textContent = quizAnswers[key].correct ? 'Correct!' : 'Not quite. The correct answer is highlighted above.';
        }
      });
      updateReadinessScore();
    }
  } catch(e) {}
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// SAVE STATE
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function saveState() {
  try {
    const checks = [];
    document.querySelectorAll('.checklist-item').forEach((item, idx) => {
      if (item.classList.contains('checked')) checks.push(idx);
    });
    localStorage.setItem('opwell-masterclass', JSON.stringify({
      surgery: selectedSurgery,
      completed: Array.from(completedModules),
      checks: checks
    }));
  } catch(e) {}
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// SAVE & RESTORE PROGRESS VIA URL
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
function saveProgress() {
  var state = {
    s: selectedSurgery || '',
    m: Array.from(completedModules),
    c: {},
    a: localStorage.getItem('mc_access') || ''
  };
  document.querySelectorAll('.checklist-item').forEach(function(item, i) {
    if (item.classList.contains('checked')) state.c[i] = 1;
  });
  var encoded = btoa(JSON.stringify(state)).replace(/\+/g,'-').replace(/\//g,'_').replace(/=+$/,'');
  var url = window.location.origin + window.location.pathname + '#p=' + encoded;
  navigator.clipboard.writeText(url).then(function() {
    alert('Progress link copied! Paste it into a text to yourself or bookmark it to resume on any device.');
  }).catch(function() {
    prompt('Copy this link to resume on any device:', url);
  });
}

function restoreFromURL() {
  var hash = window.location.hash;
  if (!hash || !hash.startsWith('#p=')) return false;
  try {
    var encoded = hash.substring(3);
    var state = JSON.parse(atob(encoded.replace(/-/g,'+').replace(/_/g,'/')));
    if (state.a) localStorage.setItem('mc_access', state.a);
    if (state.s) selectSurgery(state.s, true);
    if (state.m && state.m.length) {
      state.m.forEach(function(m) { completedModules.add(m); });
      completedModules.forEach(m => {
        var card = document.querySelector('[data-module="' + m + '"]');
        if (card) card.classList.add('completed');
      });
    }
    if (state.c) {
      var items = document.querySelectorAll('.checklist-item');
      Object.keys(state.c).forEach(function(i) {
        if (items[parseInt(i)]) items[parseInt(i)].classList.add('checked');
      });
    }
    history.replaceState(null, '', window.location.pathname);
    return true;
  } catch(e) { return false; }
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
// INIT
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
(function init() {
  // Wrap tables in scroll containers for mobile
  document.querySelectorAll('.module-body .data-table').forEach(function(table) {
    if (table.parentNode.classList.contains('table-scroll-wrap')) return;
    var wrap = document.createElement('div');
    wrap.className = 'table-scroll-wrap';
    table.parentNode.insertBefore(wrap, table);
    wrap.appendChild(table);
    wrap.addEventListener('scroll', function() {
      var atEnd = wrap.scrollLeft + wrap.clientWidth >= wrap.scrollWidth - 4;
      wrap.classList.toggle('scrolled-end', atEnd);
    });
  });

  buildIntakeDots();
  restoreFromURL();
  buildProgressDots();

  // Load saved state
  try {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem('opwell-masterclass');
    if (saved) {
      const data = JSON.parse(saved);
      if (data.completed) {
        data.completed.forEach(m => completedModules.add(m));
        completedModules.forEach(m => {
          const card = document.querySelector(`[data-module="${m}"]`);
          if (card) card.classList.add('completed');
        });
      }
      if (data.checks) {
        data.checks.forEach(idx => {
          const items = document.querySelectorAll('.checklist-item');
          if (items[idx]) items[idx].classList.add('checked');
        });
      }
    }
  } catch(e) {}

  // Load intake data if exists (returning user)
  try {
    const savedIntake = localStorage.getItem('mc_intake');
    if (savedIntake) {
      intakeData = JSON.parse(savedIntake);
      document.getElementById('intakeSection').style.display = 'none';
      applyPersonalization(intakeData);
      renderProfileSummary(intakeData);
      document.getElementById('profileSummary').style.display = 'block';
      document.getElementById('readinessSection').style.display = 'flex';
    }
  } catch(e) {}

  loadQuizState();
  updateProgress();
})();
</script>

</div><!-- end masterclass-content -->

<script>
function validateAccess() {
  var code = document.getElementById('access-code-input').value.trim();
  var errorEl = document.getElementById('access-error');

  if (!code) { errorEl.textContent = 'Please enter your access code.'; return; }

  // Admin bypass
  if (code.toLowerCase() === 'opwell') {
    document.getElementById('access-gate').style.display = 'none';
    document.getElementById('masterclass-content').style.display = 'block';
    localStorage.setItem('mc_access', code);
    return;
  }

  // Check if code starts with MC- (valid masterclass code)
  if (!code.startsWith('MC-')) {
    errorEl.textContent = 'Invalid access code. Please check your purchase confirmation email.';
    return;
  }

  try {
    // Decode the base64url token
    var payload = code.substring(3); // remove MC-
    var decoded = JSON.parse(atob(payload.replace(/-/g,'+').replace(/_/g,'/')));
    var expiry = decoded.x;
    var now = Date.now();

    if (now > expiry) {
      errorEl.textContent = 'Your access code has expired. Your PDF download link is still active — check your original purchase email.';
      return;
    }

    // Valid! Unlock content
    document.getElementById('access-gate').style.display = 'none';
    document.getElementById('masterclass-content').style.display = 'block';
    // Save to localStorage so they don't need to re-enter
    localStorage.setItem('mc_access', code);

  } catch(e) {
    errorEl.textContent = 'Invalid access code. Please check your purchase confirmation email.';
  }
}

// Auto-unlock if they've previously entered a valid code
(function() {
  var saved = localStorage.getItem('mc_access');
  if (saved) {
    document.getElementById('access-code-input').value = saved;
    validateAccess();
  }
})();
</script>

</body>
</html>

<script>
var QS = [
  // Q1 — Disease domain
  { q: "Do you have any of these health conditions?",
    sub: "Pick the one that best describes you.",
    opts: [
      {t:"No major health conditions", ds:1, flag:null},
      {t:"Controlled high blood pressure, mild asthma, or mild sleep apnea", ds:2, flag:"Chronic condition needs pre-op review"},
      {t:"Heart disease, diabetes on insulin, kidney problems, or stroke history", ds:3, flag:"Significant medical history — physician review recommended"},
      {t:"Previous bad reaction to anesthesia or difficult intubation", ds:3, flag:"Anesthesia history — critical to review before surgery"},
    ]
  },
  // Q2 — Disease domain (meds)
  { q: "How many prescription medications do you take daily?",
    opts: [
      {t:"None", ds:0, flag:null},
      {t:"1–2", ds:0, flag:null},
      {t:"3–4", ds:1, flag:"Multiple medications need surgical timing review"},
      {t:"5 or more", ds:2, flag:"Complex medication regimen — high priority for review"},
    ]
  },
  // Q3 — Disease domain (blood thinners + GLP-1)
  { q: "Do you take any of these medications?",
    sub: "Blood thinners, aspirin, anti-inflammatories (Advil/Aleve), or weight loss injections (Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro).",
    opts: [
      {t:"None of these", ds:0, flag:null},
      {t:"Aspirin or ibuprofen occasionally", ds:1, flag:"Anti-inflammatory use — needs stop-date planning"},
      {t:"Prescription blood thinners (Eliquis, Xarelto, Warfarin)", ds:3, flag:"Blood thinners require careful surgical timing"},
      {t:"Weight loss injection (Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, Zepbound)", ds:2, flag:"GLP-1 medication requires specific pre-surgery protocol"},
    ]
  },
  // Q4 — Disease domain (weight + smoking)
  { q: "Which best describes you?",
    opts: [
      {t:"Healthy weight, non-smoker", ds:0, flag:null},
      {t:"Carrying extra weight (over 200 lbs) or recently quit smoking", ds:1, flag:"Weight or smoking history may affect anesthesia"},
      {t:"Significantly overweight (over 250 lbs) or currently smoke/vape", ds:3, flag:"Weight or active smoking significantly increases surgical risk"},
    ]
  },
  // Q5 — Surgery domain
  { q: "What type of surgery are you having?",
    opts: [
      {t:"Cosmetic or minor procedure (tummy tuck, BBL, lipo, breast aug, rhinoplasty)", ss:1, flag:null},
      {t:"Moderate surgery (gallbladder, hernia, C-section, orthopedic)", ss:2, flag:null},
      {t:"Major surgery (heart, spine, joint replacement, organ removal, bariatric)", ss:3, flag:"Major surgery — pre-op optimization strongly recommended"},
      {t:"Not sure yet", ss:2, flag:null},
    ]
  },
  // Q6 — Surgery domain (location)
  { q: "Where is your surgery happening?",
    opts: [

      {t:"Considering surgery abroad", ss:1, mt:true, flag:"Considering surgery abroad — safety planning recommended"},
    ]

      {t:"Most days — it's affecting my sleep or daily life", as:3, flag:"Significant anxiety — mental wellness support recommended"},

    ]
